Name: Glow Filter
Description: Applies a multi-scale glow (light bleeding) post-processing effect that enhances bright areas by blurring and amplifying luminance. Typically used for emissive materials, UI highlights, and stylized lighting effects.

Fields.Name: Enable Glow
Fields.Description: Enables or disables the glow effect. When disabled, bright regions will not produce any bloom or light bleeding.

Fields.Name: Glow Number of Levels
Fields.Description: Number of downsampled blur levels used to construct the glow pyramid. Higher values increase glow spread and softness at the cost of performance.

Fields.Name: Blur Smoothness
Fields.Description: Controls the smoothness of the blur kernel across glow levels. Higher values reduce harsh transitions and produce a softer glow falloff.

Fields.Name: Final Color Scale
Fields.Description: Overall intensity multiplier applied to the final composited glow result. Controls how strongly the glow affects the final image.

Fields.Name: Blur Sample Scale
Fields.Description: Scales the sampling radius used during glow blur passes. Higher values produce wider, more diffused glow.

Fields.Name: Blur RGB Scale
Fields.Description: Per-channel scaling applied to the RGB components of the glow blur. Used to tint or emphasize color channels in the glow contribution.

Fields.Name: Blur Alpha Scale
Fields.Description: Controls how alpha contributes to the glow blur accumulation. Higher values increase the influence of opacity in glow shaping.
